acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] tailnet_proxy builds without daemon-internal imports
- [ ] Binary behavior preserved
- [ ] Frame/protocol types remain typed (no string codecs)
closed_reason: tailnet_proxy imports only beads-core/beads-daemon-core types; verified with import scan and cargo check --bin tailnet_proxy
description:
**Problem**
`tailnet_proxy` imports daemon repl frame internals directly.
design: Switch binary imports to shared crate boundary and keep frame/protocol typing explicit.
title: Retarget tailnet_proxy binary to shared daemon-core/surface types

acceptance_criteria: - [ ] Invalid cross-store states are unrepresentable via types\n- [ ] Replay/checkpoint/replication critical paths no longer rely on warning-only or side-channel invariants\n- [ ] Foundational dependency order enforced in bead graph
closed_reason: All structural correctness and data-loss hardening children are closed; replay/index/checkpoint/replication/session invariants are now enforced by types and verified through full epic closeout gates.
description: Umbrella epic for structural fixes that make data-loss and correctness-invalid states unrepresentable across WAL/index/replay/checkpoint/replication/session boundaries. Guided by docs/philosophy/type_design.md, trait_design.md, error_design.md, and scatter.md.
design: Hard-cutover only. Prefer invariant-bearing aggregate types, typestate transitions, and explicit effect boundaries over procedural sequencing.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Epic closeout audit: all 22 child beads are closed. Final child closed in jj change cca32f54 / rwykkyyy (). Final closeout gates passed on the completed epic tree: cargo fmt --all --check; just dylint; cargo clippy --all-features -- -D warnings; cargo test; cargo xtest; cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness. Adversarial review was batched throughout the epic and iterated to green; final bd-9phx closeout was explicitly rerun with Einstein and Huygens after fixing LabelRemove.ctx max-counter accounting, adding rebuild/catch-up proofs, and hardening the mutation-span test against close-order races under parallel test execution.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content: Correction: final child closed in jj change cca32f54 / rwykkyyy with message 'bd-9phx: remove allocator meta fsync hotpath'.

notes.author: darin@book
notes.content:
PR review follow-up: two child beads reopened during PR #92 review and are now reclosed.

Follow-up fixes:
- bd-5t0e -> /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs @ 4d48550a (change zypzxorp): fresh `PidMissing` offline unlocks now respect lease freshness unless forced.
- bd-jplw -> /Users/darin/src/personal/beads-rs @ f8d37480 (change oprlppmq): surviving stale `meta.pending.json` markers no longer override newer committed store metadata or discard WAL-index state.

Fresh follow-up verification:
- cargo fmt --all --check
- cargo test -p beads-daemon --features test-harness
- cargo clippy -p beads-daemon --all-features -- -D warnings
- just dylint

Adversarial review rerun:
- Huygens: clean
- Einstein: one policy tradeoff noted on bd-5t0e, but no additional correctness bugs accepted

title: P0: Structural correctness and data-loss hardening

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] admin_status_monotonic_under_load completes within a realistic bound on typical dev machines and CI.
- [ ] The test either uses a deadline proportional to workload or documents a tight SLA and enforces it intentionally.
- [ ] cargo test --test integration --features slow-tests passes reliably.
description:
**Problem**
admin_status_monotonic_under_load fails with "load generator did not finish within 3s" at tests/integration/daemon/admin_status.rs:126. This failed in both runs: default integration suite and slow-tests suite. That suggests either a real performance regression or a too-tight timeout for current runtime behavior.

**Repro**
- cargo test --test integration (failed on 2026-01-15)
- cargo test --test integration --features slow-tests (failed on 2026-01-15)

**Files**
- tests/integration/daemon/admin_status.rs
- tests/integration/fixtures/load_gen.rs
- src/daemon/* (mutation/apply/IPC throughput under load)
design: Determine whether the load generator is actually slow (throughput regression) or the test’s 3s deadline is unrealistic. Add timing/logging to record generator throughput, daemon startup time, and status sampling timing. If behavior is correct but slower, adjust the test to wait on completion (with a higher cap based on configured workload), or reduce workload size. If there is a regression, profile the mutation path and fix the bottleneck.
title: Integration test failure: admin_status_monotonic_under_load timeout
Admin doctor misreads relative wal.sqlite segment_path as missing file
